title: ficus theophrastoides var. angustifolia
intro: Ficus theophrastoides var. angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved fig, is a plant species belonging to the ficus genus and is native to Asia. The leaves and roots of Ficus theophrastoides var. angustifolia have been used in traditional medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Ficus theophrastoides var. angustifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for its medicinal uses, and in some cases, its leaves can be made into a poultice to apply topically.
